subject: Computer Networking
title: Networking in Space
description: A quick, practical guide to Networking in Space.

hook.text: Mars is 20 light-minutes away. TCP can't work — a 40-minute wait per acknowledgment is unworkable.

buildup.text: DTN stores data at each hop until the next node is reachable. Built for intermittent, lossy links.

discovery.text: NASA tested DTN on the ISS. It handles disconnections gracefully, unlike TCP.

twist.text: Interplanetary links face huge signal loss. A Mars signal is billions of times weaker than cellular.

climax.text: As we reach the Moon and Mars, we need an interplanetary internet. Design is underway now.

punchline.text: Space networking rewrites every rule. New protocols for a new frontier.

quiz.question: Why can't TCP work well for Mars-to-Earth communication?
options: The 40-minute round trip for acknowledgments makes TCP impractical
Mars doesn't have enough bandwidth for TCP packets
